methods.description: Process for recording geographic positions and measuring water temperature, pH, conductivity, and dissolved oxygen in situ at various springs.

steps.description: In Auvergne, the geographic position of the sites was recorded using a DGPS Trimble Geo7x.
steps.searchable_summary: record geographic position using DGPS Trimble Geo7x

steps.description: The water temperature, pH and conductivity were measured in situ with a WTW Multiline P4, and the dissolved oxygen with an oximeter Ysi ProODO.
steps.searchable_summary: measure water temperature, pH, conductivity, and dissolved oxygen in situ

steps.description: In Sardinia, the geographic position of the springs was recorded using a GPS Garmin eTrex Vista HCx.
steps.searchable_summary: record geographic position using GPS Garmin eTrex Vista HCx

steps.description: The water temperature was measured in situ using a digital thermometer (Temp 7 RTD basic with immersion probe PT100) in the warmer springs.
steps.searchable_summary: measure water temperature using digital thermometer

steps.description: In the cooler springs, water temperature, pH and conductivity were measured using a multiparameter probe (YSI ProPlus).
steps.searchable_summary: measure water temperature, pH, and conductivity using YSI ProPlus

methods.description: Laboratory analysis of water samples using high-pressure ion chromatography and other standard methods to determine various hydrochemical variables.

steps.description: The water samples were analysed in the laboratory using the high-pressure ion chromatography technique following filtration with Whatmann GF/C filters.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze water samples using high-pressure ion chromatography

steps.description: The Thermo Scientific Dionex ICS1100 and Thermo Scientific Dionex DX120 systems were used for the analyses of cations and anions, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze cations and anions using Dionex ICS1100 and DX120

steps.description: The water samples were analysed in the laboratory following standard methods reported by APAT & IRSA-CNR (2003), APHA (1998) and Strickland & Parsons (1972).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze water samples using standard methods

steps.description: Dissolved oxygen was analysed using the Winkler method (Winkler, 1888).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze dissolved oxygen using Winkler method

methods.description: Preparation and analysis of diatom samples using oxidation, light microscopy, and scanning electron microscopy to identify species.

steps.description: A part of the diatom samples (50 mL) were treated by an oxidation process on a heating plate with H2O2 (30% v/v), and, if necessary, HCl (37% v/v) was added to remove carbonates.
steps.searchable_summary: oxidize diatom samples with H2O2 and HCl

steps.description: Cleaned diatoms were mounted on microscope slides using Styrax (refractive index = 1.59) and Naphrax (refractive index = 1.73) resins.
steps.searchable_summary: mount cleaned diatoms on slides with Styrax and Naphrax

steps.description: Diatom observations and counts were performed using light microscopy (LM, Zeiss Axiovert 10 and Leica DM2700M microscopes equipped with phase-contrast and micrometric scale) at 1000× magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: observe and count diatoms using light microscopy

steps.description: Cleaned diatoms from the same treated samples were also mounted on aluminium stubs, coated with platinum, and analysed by a scanning electron microscope (SEM, Hitachi SU-70).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze diatoms using SEM

steps.description: Diatom species were identified according to various literature sources.
steps.searchable_summary: identify diatom species using literature

methods.description: Statistical analysis of diatom count data to examine assemblage structure, diversity, and relationships with environmental variables.

steps.description: Diatom count data from the epilithon and epipelon samples, analysed separately for springs in Sardinia, were integrated in a single data set and then mediated to have homogeneous data comparable with those from Auvergne.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate and mediate diatom count data

steps.description: The structure of diatom assemblages was examined by species richness (SR), the Shannon-Wiener diversity index (H) and Pielou's evenness index (J), calculated using OMNIDIA 6.0 software.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate species richness, diversity, and evenness indices

steps.description: The similarity among assemblages was analysed using non-metric multidimensional scaling ordination (nMDS).
steps.searchable_summary: perform nMDS analysis on diatom assemblages

steps.description: The significance of the differences was validated by a one-way analysis of similarities (ANOSIM).
steps.searchable_summary: validate differences using ANOSIM

steps.description: The percentage level of similarity of diatom assemblages and the percentage contribution of each species to the differences were determined using SIMPER analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform SIMPER analysis on diatom assemblages

steps.description: Relationships among environmental variables and patterns of sampling sites were analysed using a Principal Component Analysis (PCA).
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA on environmental variables

steps.description: Relationships between diatom species and environmental variables were explored by a Canonical Correspondence Analysis (CCA).
steps.searchable_summary: perform CCA on diatom species and environmental variables

methods.description: Collection of diatom and water samples for physical and chemical analyses from springs in Auvergne and Sardinia.

steps.description: Diatom samples and water samples for physical and chemical analyses were simultaneously collected in winter in eight springs in Auvergne and in eight springs in Sardinia.
steps.searchable_summary: collect diatom and water samples from springs

steps.description: Diatoms were collected on rock/cobbles and fine sediments using a hard-bristled toothbrush and glass tubes.
steps.searchable_summary: collect diatoms using toothbrush and glass tubes

steps.description: All diatom samples were preserved in 100 mL polyethylene bottles and fixed in situ with a formaldehyde solution (4% v/v).
steps.searchable_summary: preserve diatom samples with formaldehyde

steps.description: Water samples were collected using 1-L polyethylene bottles and were analysed in the laboratory within 24 h from sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and analyze water samples within 24 hours

steps.description: In the springs of Sardinia, water samples were also collected in 150 mL glass bottles and immediately fixed in situ for the analysis of dissolved oxygen in the laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and fix water samples for dissolved oxygen analysis
